input.description: Inspect paper start mechanism and status

result.output:
=== paper start --help ===
Launch an agent wired to the local paperd proxy. Validates paperd running + auth healthy, applies agent-specific proxy configuration, and exec's the named agent. Supported agents: `claude`, `codex`, and `pi`.

Args after `--` are passed through to the agent unchanged, so `paper start claude -- --dangerously-skip-permissions` runs `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` with proxy config set.

Usage: paper start [OPTIONS] [AGENT] [AGENT_ARGS]...

Arguments:
  [AGENT]
          The agent to launch (e.g. `claude`, `codex`, or `pi`). Required — bare `paper start` errors with usage guidance

  [AGENT_ARGS]...
          Trailing args to pass through to the agent. Captured after `--` so flags don't collide with paper's own

Options:
      --org-slug <ORG_SLUG>
          Active org slug for org-scoped operations (per-invocation override).
          
          Not persisted to `daemon.toml` — the active org is owned by `credentials.json` (whoami over the control socket). This flag scopes a single command without re-running `paper org select`.
          
          [env: PAPER_ORG_SLUG=]

      --gateway-id <GATEWAY_ID>
          Active gateway id for proxy routing (per-invocation override).
          
          Not persisted to `daemon.toml` — the primary gateway is owned by the `[[gateways]]` table. Use this flag to scope a single tapes subcommand to a non-primary gateway.
          
          [env: PAPER_GATEWAY_ID=]

      --backend-id <BACKEND_ID>
          Active backend id (per-invocation override). Currently unused — retained for forward compatibility with subcommands that may surface backend selection
          
          [env: PAPER_BACKEND_ID=]

      --tenant-api-url <TENANT_API_URL>
          Override the tenant-api URL for this invocation. Maps onto [`paper_config::Overrides::tenant_api_url`]
          
          [env: PAPER_TENANT_API_URL=]

      --auth-api <AUTH_API>
          WorkOS auth API hostname. Maps onto [`paper_config::Overrides::auth_api`]
          
          [env: PAPER_AUTH_API=]

      --client-id <CLIENT_ID>
          WorkOS application client ID. Maps onto [`paper_config::Overrides::client_id`]
          
          [env: PAPER_CLIENT_ID=]

      --authkit-domain <AUTHKIT_DOMAIN>
          Hosted AuthKit domain. Maps onto [`paper_config::CliConfig::authkit_domain`]
          
          [env: PAPER_AUTHKIT_DOMAIN=]

      --environment <ENVIRONMENT>
          Environment defaults bundle: `production` (default) or `staging`. Selects per-environment defaults for `client_id`, `authkit_domain`, and `tenant_api_url`. Explicit overrides above still win
          
          [env: PAPER_ENVIRONMENT=]

      --disable-telemetry <DISABLE_TELEMETRY>
          Disable telemetry events for this invocation. Maps onto [`paper_config::CliConfig::disable_telemetry`]

content.text: Let me confirm exactly what `paper start claude` injects into Claude's environment — that's the crux of whether the two collide.

echo; echo "=== grep paper binary for ANTHROPIC/proxy env names ===" && strings /usr/local/bin/paper 2>/dev/null | grep -iE '^ANTHROPIC_|_BASE_URL$|ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL|ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN|HTTPS_PROXY|ANTHROPIC_API_KEY' | sort -u | head -30
input.description: Determine env vars paper injects for claude

result.output:
=== paper shell zsh ===
# Paper shell integration
claude() {
  printf '\033[1m→ Using paper start claude\033[0m\n' >&2
  command paper start claude -- "$@"
}

codex() {
  # Auth subcommands do an OAuth/browser flow against OpenAI directly and
  # must not be gated on paperd health or a ready route — pass them
  # straight through to codex so `codex login` works during first setup.
  case "$1" in
    login|logout)
      command codex "$@"
      return
      ;;
  esac
  printf '\033[1m→ Using paper start codex\033[0m\n' >&2
  command paper start codex -- "$@"
}

pi() {
  # Management/auth-like subcommands must reach pi directly; `paper start`
  # requires healthy paperd routing before launching the agent.
  case "$1" in
    install|remove|uninstall|update|list|config|login|logout)
      command pi "$@"
      return
      ;;
  esac
  printf '\033[1m→ Using paper start pi\033[0m\n' >&2
  command paper start pi -- "$@"
}

#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
# Post-commit hook: condense session data if commit has Entire-Checkpoint trailer
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git post-commit 2>/dev/null || true; else :; fi

if [ -x "$_entire_hook_dir/post-commit.pre-entire" ]; then
    "$_entire_hook_dir/post-commit.pre-entire" "$@"

content.text:
I dug into both. Here's the complete picture — and the short version is **they don't clash; they intercept Claude at two completely different layers and actually compose cleanly.**

## entire — session/checkpoint recorder (hook-based, observe-only)

`entire enable` wired itself into Claude at **two hook layers**, plus a git ref. It never touches network or env.

**1. Claude Code lifecycle hooks** → `.claude/settings.json`
It merged its hooks into your existing file (and preserved your beads `bd prime` SessionStart/PreCompact entries). Every Claude event calls back to entire:
- `SessionStart` / `SessionEnd` / `Stop` / `UserPromptSubmit`
- `PreToolUse`/`PostToolUse` matched to `Task` and `TodoWrite`
- All guarded with `if ! command -v entire; then exit 0` so they silently no-op if the binary is gone.
- Added a permission deny: `Read(./.entire/metadata/**)`.

**2. Git hooks** → installed into the *active* hooksPath, which is **trunk's**, not `.git/hooks/`
This is the part that confused you. Your repo has `core.hooksPath = ~/.cache/trunk/.../git-hooks`, so `.git/hooks/` is dead (only `.sample` files there). entire correctly detected that, and in trunk's dir it:
- renamed trunk's `post-commit`→`post-commit.pre-entire` and `pre-push`→`pre-push.pre-entire` (those "backed up" messages)
- dropped its own `post-commit`/`pre-push` that run `entire hooks git <event>` **then chain back** to the `.pre-entire` (trunk) hook.

So trunk + entire both run on commit/push — chained, not clobbered.

**3.** Orphan ref `refs/heads/entire/checkpoints/v1` stores session metadata in git. Writes are confined to `.entire/`, `.claude/settings.json`, and `~/.config/entire/`. Telemetry is off (`.entire/settings.json` → `telemetry:false`).

## paper — LLM gateway proxy (network-based)

Totally different mechanism. `paperd` runs as a launchd daemon on `127.0.0.1:51539`, upstream `https://fnn21.papercompute.run/`.
- `paper start claude` validates the daemon + auth, sets **`ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`** to the local proxy, and `exec`s `claude`. Claude's model API traffic now routes through paperd → papercompute.
- `paper shell zsh` installs a `claude() { paper start claude -- "$@"; }` shell function — so if you've eval'd that in `.zshrc`, plain `claude` *is* `paper start claude`. This is the only thing intercepting the literal `claude` command.
- Config lives in paper's own dir (`daemon.toml`, `credentials.json`, control socket) — zero overlap with entire's files.

## Why there's no clash

| | entire | paper |
|---|---|---|
| Layer | Agent event lifecycle + git hooks | Model HTTP traffic (env/proxy) |
| Mechanism | `.claude/settings.json` hooks, git hooks | `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` + `exec claude` |
| Touches how claude launches? | No | Yes (`claude()` shadow) |
| Touches `.claude/settings.json`? | Yes | No |
| Touches git? | Yes | No |

They're orthogonal, so they stack: run `paper start claude` (or the shadowed `claude`) → Claude launches with the proxy env → inside that session entire's hooks fire normally, because hooks are config-driven and don't care how the process was started. You get paper's gateway routing **and** entire's session tracking simultaneously.

## The handful of things actually worth watching (interactions, not conflicts)

1. **`claude` is shadowed by paper's shell function** — that's the real "interception" you noticed on the paper side. entire does *not* shadow `claude`; it only adds hooks. Both end up active.
2. **Git hooks are now 3-way chained**: trunk (owner) ← entire wraps it. If you ever run `entire disable`, confirm it restores trunk's hooks from the `.pre-entire` backups rather than leaving them orphaned.
3. **`.git/hooks/` is inert** in this repo (core.hooksPath wins) — don't look there for either tool's hooks.
4. **The one future risk**: if some future entire feature also tried to set `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` or an HTTP proxy, it would fight paper for the network layer. Today it doesn't — entire is purely hook/observability, so you're fine.

Want me to verify the `entire disable` path actually restores trunk's `.pre-entire` hooks cleanly, or check whether running both produces duplicate session/telemetry records anywhere?
yeah mess around with entire and figure out how it's setup and what it does exactly!
Let me poke at it properly — explore the command surface, the data it captures, and what's actually stored.

content.text: `entire status` already shows it's tracking *this very session*. Let me learn the real subcommand syntax and look at what it captured.

input.command:
cd /Users/elvis/Documents/ai/gtm-sdk

result.output:
========== entire session --help ==========
View and manage agent sessions tracked by Entire.

Commands:
  list     List all sessions across all worktrees
  info     Show detailed information for a specific session
  tokens   Show token usage and optimization recommendations
  stop     Stop one or more active sessions
  current  Show the active session for the current worktree
  attach   Attach an existing agent session
  adopt    Adopt an active session from another worktree
  resume   Switch to a branch and resume its session

Examples:
  entire session list                      List all sessions
  entire session info <session-id>         Show session details
  entire session info <session-id> --json  Output as JSON
  entire session tokens <session-id>       Show token usage
  entire session stop                      Interactive stop
  entire session current                   Active session for cwd
  entire session attach <session-id>       Attach an external session
  entire session adopt <session-id> --from ../repo  Adopt a moved session
  entire session resume <branch>           Resume from a branch

Usage:
  entire session [command]

Aliases:
  session, sessions

Available Commands:
  adopt       Adopt an active session from another worktree
  attach      Attach an existing agent session
  current     Show the active session for the current worktree
  info        Show detailed session information
  list        List all sessions

========== entire checkpoint --help ==========
Operations on checkpoints — the persistent records of agent work tied to commits.

Commands:
  list     List checkpoints on the current branch
  explain  Explain a checkpoint, commit, or session
  tokens   Show token usage and optimization recommendations
  search   Search checkpoints (semantic + keyword)

Examples:
  entire checkpoint list
  entire checkpoint explain <id|sha>
  entire checkpoint tokens <id>
  entire checkpoint search "fix login"

Usage:
  entire checkpoint [command]

Aliases:
  checkpoint, cp, checkpoints

Available Commands:
  explain     Explain a session, commit, or checkpoint
  list        List checkpoints on the current branch
  search      Search checkpoints, commits, and sessions using semantic and keyword matching
  tokens      Show token usage and optimization recommendations for a checkpoint

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for checkpoint

Use "entire checkpoint [command] --help" for more information about a command.

========== entire agent --help ==========
Manage agent integrations in this repository.

Commands:
  list     Show installed and available agents
  add      Install hooks for an agent
  remove   Uninstall hooks for an agent

Examples:
  entire agent
  entire agent list
  entire agent add claude-code
  entire agent remove claude-code

Usage:
  entire agent [flags]
  entire agent [command]

Available Commands:
  add         Install hooks for an agent
  list        List installed and available agents
  remove      Uninstall hooks for an agent

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for agent

Use "entire agent [command] --help" for more information about a command.

========== entire activity --help ==========
Display your activity overview, repository breakdown, and recent commits from entire.io

Usage:
  entire activity [flags]

Flags:
  -h, --help   help for activity

========== entire recap --help ==========
Summarize recent checkpoint activity

Usage:
  entire recap [flags]

Flags:
      --90                   Rolling 90 days
      --agent string         Agent id to show, or all (default "all")
      --color string         Color output: auto, always, or never (default "auto")
      --day                  Today only (default)
  -h, --help                 help for recap
      --insecure-http-auth   Allow plain-HTTP auth (local dev only)
      --month                This calendar month
      --static               Print static output instead of opening the interactive recap
      --view string          Which columns to show: you, team, or both (default "both")
      --week                 Last 7 days

========== entire dispatch --help ==========
Generate a dispatch summarizing recent agent work.

Examples:
  entire dispatch
  entire dispatch --local --all-branches
  entire dispatch --repos entireio/cli
  entire dispatch --voice neutral

Usage:
  entire dispatch [flags]

Flags:
      --all-branches    include every existing local branch (--local only; renamed or deleted branches are skipped)
  -h, --help            help for dispatch
      --local           generate via the locally-installed agent CLI instead of the Entire server
      --repos strings   cloud repo slugs, up to 5 (for example entireio/cli)
      --since string    time window (Go duration, relative time, or ISO date) (default "7d")
      --until string    window end time (defaults to now)
      --voice string    voice preset name or literal description

========== entire labs --help ==========
Labs

These are newer Entire workflows we are actively refining. They are available
to try now, but details may change based on feedback.

Available experimental commands:
  entire review             Run a multi-agent review against the current branch
  entire investigate        Run a multi-agent investigation against a topic, issue, or seed doc
  entire import claude-code Import existing Claude Code transcripts as local, read-only history
  entire tokens             Analyze experimental token usage diagnostics
  entire tokens profile     Aggregate token usage across committed checkpoints
  entire org                Manage Entire organizations (create, list)
  entire project            Manage Entire projects (create, list)
  entire repo               Manage Entire repositories (create, list, get, delete)
  entire grant              Manage access grants and org membership (org, project, repo)
  entire blame              Show which lines came from Entire checkpoints
  entire why                Show why a line exists (commit, checkpoint, prompt, session)

Try:
  entire review --help
  entire investigate --help
  entire tokens --help
  entire tokens profile --help
  entire org --help
  entire project --help
  entire repo --help
  entire grant --help
  entire blame --help
  entire why --help

Usage:
  entire labs [flags]

Flags:

content.text:
Done poking — I have the full picture now, including a live demo of it tracking *this* conversation. Here's exactly what entire is and how it's wired.

## What entire actually is

A **git-adjacent recorder for AI-agent work**. It watches your coding-agent sessions, captures the prompts + full transcript + the files the agent changed, and ties each unit of work ("checkpoint") to your real git commits. Think "version control / blame layer for what the AI did." Cloud features (team activity, recap, dispatch, multi-agent review) need an account — you're currently **local-only, not logged in, telemetry off**.

It's v0.7.8, Go binary at `~/.local/bin/entire`, and supports 8 agents (claude-code ✓ installed; codex, copilot-cli, cursor, gemini, opencode, droid, pi available).

## How `entire enable` wired itself in (4 places)

1. **Claude Code hooks** → `.claude/settings.json` (the primary capture path). Fires on `SessionStart/End`, `Stop`, `UserPromptSubmit`, `Pre/PostToolUse`(Task, TodoWrite), `PreCompact` → each runs `entire hooks claude-code <event>`.
2. **Git hooks** → installed into the *active* hooksPath (trunk's dir), chained over trunk's via the `.pre-entire` backups. `post-commit` + `pre-push` call `entire hooks git <event>`; post-commit condenses session data into any commit carrying an `Entire-Checkpoint` trailer.
3. **Orphan git ref** `refs/heads/entire/checkpoints/v1` — a separate branch (currently just an empty "Initialize metadata ref" commit) where checkpoint metadata gets committed, keeping it off your working branches.
4. **`.entire/` working dir** — `settings.json` (enabled, telemetry:false), `logs/entire.log`, `metadata/<session>/{prompt.txt,full.jsonl}`, `tmp/pre-prompt-<session>.json`. Its `.gitignore` excludes `tmp/ metadata/ logs/ settings.local.json redactors/local/`.

## The capture lifecycle (reconstructed from its own logs)

- **SessionStart** → registers the session, keyed to your Claude Code transcript file (`~/.claude/projects/.../<uuid>.jsonl`), records the model, spins up a "shadow session."
- **UserPromptSubmit** → writes `tmp/pre-prompt-<sid>.json`, which snapshots: `session_id`, `timestamp`, **`untracked_files`** (git state at prompt time), and **`transcript_offset`** (the line in the transcript where this turn starts).
- **TurnStart** → phase `idle → active`.
- **TurnEnd** → waits for a transcript "flush sentinel," reads the transcript from the saved offset, diffs files against the snapshot. **Files changed → create a checkpoint; no changes → "skipping checkpoint."** (That's why I have 0 checkpoints — I've only read, never written this session.)
- A checkpoint is later bound to your actual commit via the `Entire-Checkpoint` trailer + the post-commit hook. Mode is **manual-commit** — it never auto-commits; it rides *your* commits.

## Live demo — what it's recording *right now*

`entire status` / `session list` show it silently tracking **two concurrent sessions in this one worktree**:
- `9fae47dd…` — *this* conversation ("yeah mess around with entire…")
- `ba68dc03…` — a **second Claude session you have open** in the same repo, pasting Entire's own install docs ("…nstalling the CLI and enabling Entire…")

It has already written a **188K `full.jsonl` transcript** of this session to `.entire/metadata/`.

## Gotchas worth knowing

1. **It mislabels the model.** This session is recorded as `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`, but I'm Opus 4.8. The SessionStart hook just stores whatever model field CC passes at init (the lightweight model CC uses for session bootstrap). So per-session model attribution is unreliable, and `session tokens` reports "Token data unavailable" — CC doesn't feed token counts to the hook.
2. **`session current` breaks with concurrent sessions** — it returned `ba68dc03` (the newer one) even though I'm in `9fae47dd`. Two agents in one checkout confuse "current."
3. **It keeps a plaintext copy of your whole transcript** in `.entire/metadata/.../full.jsonl`. It's gitignored (won't be committed) but it's unencrypted on disk. entire guards it with a `Read(./.entire/metadata/**)` deny in `.claude/settings.json` — that rule actually blocked one of my commands mid-investigation. The unused `redactors/local/` gitignore entry hints redaction is planned but not on.
4. **`.git/hooks/` is inert** here (core.hooksPath → trunk), so entire correctly installed into trunk's dir instead — easy to miss if you go looking.

## The payoff features (once checkpoints exist + you log in)

- `entire blame` — which lines came from an agent checkpoint
- `entire why <line>` — trace a line of code back to the **prompt + session** that produced it
- `entire checkpoint search "…"` — semantic search across past agent work
- `entire labs review` / `investigate` — multi-agent review/investigation of the branch
- `entire dispatch` / `recap` / `activity` — summaries of recent agent work (cloud)

Net for you: it's purely a recorder. It doesn't touch your code, your model traffic, or how Claude launches — so (as established) it composes fine with paper. The one thing to decide is whether you're comfortable with full transcripts sitting in `.entire/metadata/` on disk; if not, `entire configure` or the redactors mechanism is where you'd lock that down.

Want me to test the checkpoint flow end-to-end (make a throwaway file edit + commit so it generates a real checkpoint on the orphan ref), then show what `entire blame`/`why`/`explain` actually produce?

yes let's do this:
Want me to test the checkpoint flow end-to-end (make a throwaway file edit + commit so it generates a real checkpoint on the orphan ref), then show what entire blame/why/explain actually produce?
"yes let's do this" — let me run the full experiment. First I'll read the commit-time hooks (so I can interpret what happens), and set up a throwaway branch so `main` stays clean.

input.command:
HP="/Users/elvis/.cache/trunk/repos/cd9513b395b063d7764d817334290c23/git-hooks"
for h in prepare-commit-msg post-commit pre-push commit-msg post-rewrite; do
  echo "========== $h =========="
  cat "$HP/$h" 2>/dev/null
  echo
done

result.output:
========== prepare-commit-msg ==========
#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git prepare-commit-msg "$1" "$2" 2>/dev/null || true; else :; fi

========== post-commit ==========
#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
# Post-commit hook: condense session data if commit has Entire-Checkpoint trailer
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git post-commit 2>/dev/null || true; else :; fi
# Chain: run pre-existing hook
_entire_hook_dir="$(dirname "$0")"
if [ -x "$_entire_hook_dir/post-commit.pre-entire" ]; then
    "$_entire_hook_dir/post-commit.pre-entire" "$@"
fi

========== pre-push ==========
#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
# Pre-push hook: push session logs alongside user's push
# $1 is the remote name (e.g., "origin")
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git pre-push "$1"; else :; fi
# Chain: run pre-existing hook
_entire_hook_dir="$(dirname "$0")"
if [ -x "$_entire_hook_dir/pre-push.pre-entire" ]; then
    "$_entire_hook_dir/pre-push.pre-entire" "$@"
fi

========== commit-msg ==========
#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
# Commit-msg hook: strip trailer if no user content (allows aborting empty commits)
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git commit-msg "$1" || true; else printf '%s\n' '[entire] Entire CLI is enabled but not installed or not on PATH. Skipping Entire Git hook; continuing. Installation guide: https://docs.entire.io/cli/installation#installation-methods' >&2 || :; fi

========== post-rewrite ==========
#!/bin/sh
# Entire CLI hooks
# Post-rewrite hook: remap session linkage after amend/rebase rewrites
if command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then entire hooks git post-rewrite "$1" 2>/dev/null || true; else :; fi
